article_title: Singapore Opens World's Tallest Vertical Farm as the City-State Recalibrates Its Food Security Strategy

content_preview.title: Singapore Opens World's Tallest Vertical Farm as the City-State Recalibrates Its Food Security Strategy
content_preview.summary: Greenphyto's S$80 million, AI-powered indoor facility in Jurong West can produce 2,000 metric tons of vegetables per year, arriving as the global vertical farming market hits $7.5 billion and Singapore revises its food security goals.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## A 23-Meter Tower of Lettuce and Kai Lan

Singapore's Greenphyto opened the world's tallest indoor vertical farm on January 7 in the Jurong West industrial district, a [23.3-meter-high, 118-meter-long hydroponic complex](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-01-07/world-s-tallest-vertical-farm-opens-in-import-reliant-singapore) that cost S$80 million (approximately US$62 million) and took 14 years to develop.
